name: Napa Unified School District Cheese Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Place warm water, sugar, and powdered milk in a very large mixing bowl. Stir well until blended and dissolved.
2. Add the dry yeast to the mixture and stir gently, blending out any lumps. Let the mixture become bubbly.
3. Gradually add 40 pounds of flour and margarine/butter to the mixture. Mix thoroughly and continue adding flour until you achieve the consistency of bread dough. Knead until the dough is elastic.
4. Divide the dough into two 1 lb balls.
5. Sprinkle a little flour on a clean, floured surface and roll each ball into a rectangle about 1/4 inch thick.
6. Spread American cheese over the dough, overlapping slices to form three layers of cheese.
7. Roll up the rectangles tightly into loaves and fold the ends to keep the cheese from melting out.
8. Place the rolls on parchment paper or in a loaf pan. Allow them to rise until about double in size in a warm place, covered.
9. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) for conventional ovens, or 300°F (150°C) for convection ovens. Bake for approximately 20-30 minutes, or until golden brown. Slice and serve warm.
This recipe makes enough dough for about 44 loaves. To adjust for two loaves, divide all the ingredients by approximately 22.
